name: Chocolate Gingerbread Sleighs
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, beat brown sugar and butter until light and fluffy.
2. Add molasses and egg yolk; blend well.
3. Lightly spoon flour into measuring cup; level off. Add flour, cocoa, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon; mix well.
4. Cover with plastic wrap; refrigerate 2 hours for easier handling.
5. Lightly grease cookie sheets.
6. Heat oven to 350°F.
7. On a floured surface, roll out half of the dough to 3/16 inch thickness. Keep remaining dough refrigerated.
8. Using sleigh side pattern, cut 7 sleigh sides; reserve trimmings.
9. With a large floured spatula, transfer pieces to greased cookie sheets, placing them about 1 inch apart. Turn the pattern over and cut 7 additional sleigh sides. Place each about 1 inch apart on greased cookie sheet.
10. Bake at 350°F for 6-9 minutes or until set. Remove from cookie sheets and cool.
11. Meanwhile, roll reserved trimmings to 3/16 inch thickness. Using a square sleigh pattern, cut 14 squares. Bake and cool as directed for sleigh sides.
12. In a small bowl, beat powdered sugar, cream of tartar, and egg white at low speed until blended. Beat at high speed until stiff.
13. Spoon frosting into a pastry bag with a medium writing tip.
14. Keep any remaining frosting covered with damp paper towel or plastic wrap.
15. To assemble sleighs, place one sleigh side (back side up) on a flat surface. Pipe frosting along one edge of each of 2 squares. Attach the squares to the sleigh, making sure both sides are even.
16. Hold in place a few seconds until frosting is set. Repeat with remaining sleigh sides and squares to make 7 sleighs.
17. Wrap pastry bag to keep from drying out. Let sleighs stand 1 hour or until frosting is completely dry.
18. To decorate, using frosting in the pastry bag, attach a candy cane along the outside bottom edge on each side of each sleigh to resemble runners. Using a small writing tip, write name on each side of the sleigh.
19. Decorate with frosting and candies as desired.
20. To serve, fill sleighs with candies.
